name: Jammu and Kashmir (Indian Union Territory)
summary: Jammu and Kashmir (J&K) is a South Asian mixed common-law/Islamic-personal-law Indian Union Territory — structurally distinctive globally as the only state-to-Union-Territory constitutional demotion in Indian constitutional history (Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act 2019 effective 31 October 2019, which simultaneously abrogated the Article 370 special-status framework that had operated since 1954 and split the former state of Jammu and Kashmir into two Union Territories: J&K with a legislature and Ladakh without). The reorganisation is judicially upheld but politically contested; final Indian Supreme Court judgment in In Re Article 370 (2023) upheld the constitutional validity of the abrogation in a 5-0 unanimous Constitution Bench ruling. J&K is also one of the central jurisdictions of the broader Kashmir conflict involving Pakistani-administered Azad Kashmir + Gilgit-Baltistan and Chinese-administered Aksai Chin. Family-law framework operates under the dual Indian common-law + Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937 framework for the predominantly-Muslim (~68%) population; Hindu Marriage Act 1955 framework for ~28% Hindu population; the previously distinct J&K family-law statutes (J&K Hindu Marriage Act, J&K Muslim Personal Law Act) were replaced by Indian central statutes following the 2019 reorganisation. Parental authority and child custody operate under the Guardians and Wards Act 1890 supplemented by personal-law frameworks. The High Court of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh is the apex domestic appellate court (also covering Ladakh); final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Supreme Court of India. J&K is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. India is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party (signature 6 November 2007, never ratified) — J&K is governed by the Indian non-ratification status.

statutory_framework.title: J&K Reorganisation Act 2019
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Indian Act effective 31 October 2019 abrogating Article 370 special-status framework, abolishing the State of Jammu and Kashmir, and creating two Union Territories: J&K with a legislature and Ladakh without.

statutory_framework.title: Constitution of India Article 370
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitution of India Article 370 establishing special autonomous status for Jammu and Kashmir from 1954-2019 — abrogated by Presidential Order C.O. 272 of 5 August 2019 and J&K Reorganisation Act 2019.

statutory_framework.citation: Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937 (applicable in J&K)
statutory_framework.title: Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Muslim Personal Law Act applicable in J&K following the 2019 reorganisation — previously the J&K Muslim Personal Law Act applied.

apex_courts.name: High Court of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from J&K Union Territory and Ladakh Union Territory.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of India
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from J&K.

professional_regulators.name: Indian Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI)
professional_regulators.role: Indian Federal professional regulatory framework for clinical psychology applicable in J&K.

anonymisation_convention: J&K family-court decisions are anonymised per Indian High Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Amritsar + Dogra-Princely-State-of-J&K-+-Maharaja-Gulab-Singh framework
key_developments.description: Treaty of Amritsar 16 March 1846 establishing Dogra Princely State of Jammu and Kashmir under Maharaja Gulab Singh — substantively distinctive Trans-Himalayan Dogra-Princely-State-of-J&K-with-Hindu-ruler-+-Muslim-majority-population framework. Foundational pre-1947-Partition-+-Instrument-of-Accession framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory and Kashmir-dispute-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Instrument of Accession + Maharaja-Hari-Singh-+-conditional-accession framework
key_developments.description: Maharaja Hari Singh signed Instrument of Accession to India 26 October 1947 — substantively distinctive globally Princely-State-with-Muslim-majority-and-Hindu-ruler-acceding-to-India-with-conditional-instrument framework. Foundational substantive Kashmir-dispute framework + First Kashmir War October 1947-January 1949 affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Constitution of India Article 370 + special-autonomous-status framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of India Article 370 adopted 17 October 1949 establishing special autonomous status for Jammu and Kashmir — substantively distinctive globally only-Indian-state-with-formal-constitutional-special-autonomous-status framework. Foundational substantive 70-year Article 370 framework persisting through 2019 abrogation affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: J&K Constitution adopted + unique-state-level-constitutional-document framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of Jammu and Kashmir adopted 26 January 1957 — substantively distinctive globally only-Indian-state-with-state-level-constitutional-document-within-Indian-Federal-framework framework. Foundational substantive J&K-state-constitution framework persisting through 2019 abrogation affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Kashmir insurgency + Pandit-exodus-+-AFSPA-applicability framework
key_developments.description: Kashmir insurgency 1989-1990 onwards substantively reshaping demographic and family-law-implementation framework — substantively distinctive globally Kashmiri-Pandit-exodus-+-AFSPA-Armed-Forces-Special-Powers-Act-applicability framework. Substantive 35+ year insurgency framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory through 2019 reorganisation.

key_developments.title: India ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + J&K-applicability framework
key_developments.description: India ratified UNCRC 11 December 1992 with applicability to J&K — establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within Indian-derivative-common-law framework + J&K state-level family-law statutes co-applicability.

key_developments.title: India signs Hague Convention 1980 + Indian-non-ratification framework
key_developments.description: India signed Hague Convention 1980 6 November 2007 — substantively distinctive globally only-nuclear-armed-UN-permanent-veto-aspirant-state-never-to-ratify-Hague-Abduction-Convention framework. Substantive Indian-non-Hague-1980 framework persisting through 21st century affecting subsequent cross-border-jurisdiction-practice for J&K.

key_developments.title: Article 370 abrogation + J&K-Reorganisation-Act + state-to-Union-Territory-demotion framework
key_developments.description: Presidential Order C.O. 272 of 5 August 2019 abrogated Article 370 special-status framework — substantively distinctive globally only-state-to-Union-Territory-constitutional-demotion-in-Indian-constitutional-history framework. J&K Reorganisation Act 2019 effective 31 October 2019 abolished the State of Jammu and Kashmir and created two Union Territories: J&K with a legislature and Ladakh without. Foundational substantive post-Article-370 framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: In Re Article 370 + Indian-Supreme-Court-Constitution-Bench-5-0-unanimous-upholding framework
key_developments.description: Indian Supreme Court Constitution Bench judgment In Re Article 370 (2023) 11 December 2023 unanimously upheld the constitutional validity of the Article 370 abrogation 5-0 — substantively distinctive globally only-modern-apex-court-constitutional-validation-of-state-restructuring-decision framework. Substantive constitutional-democratic-trajectory framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Lieutenant Governor framework + UT-level family-court framework + Indian-Personal-Law applicability framework
key_developments.description: J&K Lieutenant Governor (Manoj Sinha) framework continuing through 2022 under post-Article-370 Union Territory governance framework. UT-level family-court framework substantively significant institutional context. Indian-Personal-Law applicability framework substantively significant for family-law adjudication including Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937 + Hindu Marriage Act 1955 framework. Substantively significant South Asian Union Territory institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: High Court of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh + Supreme Court — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + post-Article-370-+-Kashmir-conflict-three-nuclear-state context framework
key_developments.description: High Court of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh and Supreme Court of India continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937 + Hindu Marriage Act 1955 + Guardians and Wards Act 1890 + J&K Reorganisation Act 2019 framework in custody disputes within continuing Kashmir-conflict-three-nuclear-state context (India, Pakistan, China) + post-Article-370-Union-Territory framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Jammu and Kashmir operates a mixed Indian common-law + Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) + Hindu Personal Law framework — places J&K in the Indian Union Territory cluster.
Only state-to-Union-Territory constitutional demotion in Indian constitutional history is structurally distinctive globally — only modern formal constitutional state-to-Union-Territory demotion.
Article 370 abrogation framework (2019) is structurally distinctive globally — modern abrogation of constitutional special-status framework operating since 1954.
In Re Article 370 Indian Supreme Court 5-0 unanimous upholding (2023) is structurally distinctive — modern apex-court constitutional validation of state-restructuring decision.

Kashmir conflict integration with Pakistani-administered Azad Kashmir + Gilgit-Baltistan and Chinese-administered Aksai Chin is structurally distinctive globally — only state-level territorial conflict involving three nuclear-armed neighbouring states.

Loss of distinct J&K family-law statutes (J&K Hindu Marriage Act, J&K Muslim Personal Law Act) following 2019 reorganisation is structurally distinctive — only modern formal replacement of distinct state-level personal-law statutes by central Indian statutes.

Indian non-Hague-Convention-1980-ratification status (signed 2007, never ratified) is structurally distinctive — only nuclear-armed UN-permanent-veto-aspirant state never to ratify the Hague Abduction Convention.

sources.title: High Court of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh

editorial_notes: Jammu and Kashmir jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 5 to 10 key_developments with full Treaty-of-Amritsar-1846-to-continuing-post-Article-370 trajectory: 1846-Treaty-of-Amritsar-+-Dogra-Princely-State + 1947-Instrument-of-Accession + 1949-Article-370 + 1957-J&K-Constitution + 1990-Kashmir-insurgency-+-Pandit-exodus + 1992-India-UNCRC-ratification + 2007-India-signs-Hague-Convention-+-non-ratification + 2019-Article-370-abrogation-+-J&K-Reorganisation-Act + 2023-In-Re-Article-370-Supreme-Court-unanimous-upholding + 2024-High-Court-of-J&K-and-Ladakh-+-Supreme-Court-best-interests.

Mixed Indian common-law/Muslim Personal Law/Hindu Personal Law South Asian Union Territory (Indian Constitution + Article 370 abrogation 2019 + J&K Reorganisation Act 2019 + Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937 + Hindu Marriage Act 1955 + In Re Article 370 Supreme Court 2023 + Indian non-Hague-1980). Only state-to-Union-Territory constitutional demotion in Indian constitutional history + modern abrogation of constitutional special-status framework operating since 1954 + central jurisdiction of Kashmir conflict involving three nuclear-armed states + only nuclear-armed UN-permanent-veto-aspirant state never to ratify Hague Abduction Convention.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937 + Hindu Marriage Act 1955 + Guardians and Wards Act 1890 + J&K Reorganisation Act 2019 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins South-Asian + mixed-Indian-common-law/personal-law + Indian-Union-Territory-cluster + state-to-UT-demotion-globally-distinctive-only-in-Indian-constitutional-history + Article-370-abrogation-2019-globally-distinctive + In-Re-Article-370-Indian-Supreme-Court-5-0-unanimous-upholding-2023-only-modern-apex-court-constitutional-validation-of-state-restructuring + Kashmir-conflict-three-nuclear-state-jurisdiction-India-Pakistan-China-globally-distinctive + Treaty-of-Amritsar-1846-Dogra-Princely-State-with-Muslim-majority-+-Hindu-ruler + Instrument-of-Accession-1947-+-First-Kashmir-War + J&K-Constitution-1957-only-Indian-state-with-state-level-constitutional-document + Kashmir-insurgency-1989-1990-+-Pandit-exodus-+-AFSPA-applicability + India-signs-Hague-Convention-2007-never-ratified-only-nuclear-armed-UN-P5-aspirant-distinctive + Indian-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
